이사야 42:1.content: “Behold, my servant, whom I uphold, my chosen, in whom my soul delights: I have put my Spirit on him. He will bring justice to the nations.

이사야 42:2.content: He will not shout, nor raise his voice, nor cause it to be heard in the street.

이사야 42:3.content: He won’t break a bruised reed. He won’t quench a dimly burning wick. He will faithfully bring justice.

이사야 42:4.content: He will not fail nor be discouraged, until he has set justice in the earth, and the islands wait for his law.”

이사야 42:5.content: God Yahweh, he who created the heavens and stretched them out, he who spread out the earth and that which comes out of it, he who gives breath to its people and spirit to those who walk in it, says:

이사야 42:6.content: “I, Yahweh, have called you in righteousness. I will hold your hand. I will keep you, and make you a covenant for the people, as a light for the nations,

이사야 42:7.content: to open the blind eyes, to bring the prisoners out of the dungeon, and those who sit in darkness out of the prison.

이사야 42:8.content: “I am Yahweh. That is my name. I will not give my glory to another, nor my praise to engraved images.

이사야 42:9.content: Behold, the former things have happened and I declare new things. I tell you about them before they come up.”

이사야 42:10.content: Sing to Yahweh a new song, and his praise from the end of the earth, you who go down to the sea, and all that is therein, the islands and their inhabitants.

이사야 42:11.content: Let the wilderness and its cities raise their voices, with the villages that Kedar inhabits. Let the inhabitants of Sela sing. Let them shout from the top of the mountains!

이사야 42:12.content: Let them give glory to Yahweh, and declare his praise in the islands.

이사야 42:13.content: Yahweh will go out like a mighty man. He will stir up zeal like a man of war. He will raise a war cry. Yes, he will shout aloud. He will triumph over his enemies.

이사야 42:14.content: “I have been silent a long time. I have been quiet and restrained myself. Now I will cry out like a travailing woman. I will both gasp and pant.

이사야 42:15.content: I will destroy mountains and hills, and dry up all their herbs. I will make the rivers islands, and will dry up the pools.

이사야 42:16.content: I will bring the blind by a way that they don’t know. I will lead them in paths that they don’t know. I will make darkness light before them, and crooked places straight. I will do these things, and I will not forsake them.

이사야 42:17.content: “Those who trust in engraved images, who tell molten images, ‘You are our gods,’ will be turned back. They will be utterly disappointed.

이사야 42:18.content: “Hear, you deaf, and look, you blind, that you may see.

이사야 42:19.content: Who is blind, but my servant? Or who is as deaf as my messenger whom I send? Who is as blind as he who is at peace, and as blind as Yahweh’s servant?

이사야 42:20.content: You see many things, but don’t observe. His ears are open, but he doesn’t listen.

이사야 42:21.content: It pleased Yahweh, for his righteousness’ sake, to magnify the law and make it honorable.

이사야 42:22.content: But this is a robbed and plundered people. All of them are snared in holes, and they are hidden in prisons. They have become captives, and no one delivers, and a plunder, and no one says, ‘Restore them!’

이사야 42:23.content: Who is there among you who will give ear to this? Who will listen and hear for the time to come?

이사야 42:24.content: Who gave Jacob as plunder, and Israel to the robbers? Didn’t Yahweh, he against whom we have sinned? For they would not walk in his ways, and they disobeyed his law.

이사야 42:25.content: Therefore he poured the fierceness of his anger on him, and the strength of battle. It set him on fire all around, but he didn’t know. It burned him, but he didn’t take it to heart.”
